highlights.title: Dense annotations are easier to browse
highlights.description: When many notes sit near the same passage, cards now fan out instead of hiding in a flat stack. Bring a covered note forward when you need it without hunting through the rail.

highlights.title: The reader foundation is steadier
highlights.description: Reader navigation, annotations, progress, settings, AI, EPUB/PDF import, and responsive layouts now have broader end-to-end coverage, making future reader changes easier to validate before release.

highlights.title: Selection and search behave more consistently
highlights.description: Web articles, ebooks, and PDFs now share selection-adjustment rules, and reader search navigation uses one shared boundary. You may not see a new button, but behavior across source types should feel more even.

highlights.title: Automation no longer writes real heartbeats
highlights.description: Local and CI automation runs no longer send desktop telemetry heartbeats, so release validation does not mix test runs into real usage data.
